key_departures.impact: Signed a 4yr/$76.4M deal in 2024 but appeared in only 12 games over two seasons due to quad and knee injuries. Provided essentially zero return on investment. As of March 13, release pending — guaranteed money timing around March 16 is the hold-up, but his departure is considered a near-certainty by all reports.

Signed 4yr/$94M in 2024. Managed only 303 yards in 7 games in 2025 before a broken fibula ended his season. Status unclear but widely expected to be released. If cut, saves approximately $14M in cap space. His departure would create or escalate a WR need, though Robinson signing partially compensates.

Signed a 3yr/$30M deal with the Commanders. Was the Titans' most productive TE — 194 catches for 2,017 yards over his 4-year career. Titans deliberately chose not to re-sign him in favor of Bellinger. Partially offset by Bellinger signing, but Okonkwo was a superior receiving option.

Signed 2yr/$20M with the Colts. Was a reliable rotational edge rusher for the Titans over 3 seasons, recording 4 sacks and 33 pressures in 12 games in 2025. His loss escalates EDGE need but he was not a true starter.

Rotational DT who appeared in all 17 games in 2025 with 2 sacks and 14 pressures. Titans chose to replace him with Franklin-Myers at a much higher price point. Loss is absorbed by the Franklin-Myers signing.
Starting center released in late February after failing a physical. Departure creates a clear hole at center heading into free agency, which the team has only partially addressed via Schlottmann.
Starting safety released in late February. Had signed a 2yr/$8M deal before 2025. Departure opens a hole at safety opposite Kevin Winston Jr. and Amani Hooker.

Traded to Jets in player-for-player swap for Jermaine Johnson. Was a 2024 2nd-round pick (38th overall) who never fully fit Robert Saleh's scheme. His departure was immediately offset by Franklin-Myers and Elliott signings.
Veteran punter allowed to walk. Replaced by Townsend signing.

signings.analysis: Robinson (92 catches, 1,014 yards, 4 TDs in 2025) reunites with OC Brian Daboll and instantly becomes Cam Ward's WR1. 4yr/$70M with $38M guaranteed is a real commitment — 2 years fully guaranteed at signing is a true investment. However, he is a slot-only receiver. The Titans' WR room still lacks a true outside 'X' or deep threat. WR need drops from critical (tier 1) to moderate (tier 2) — they need an outside receiver complement to pair with Robinson, particularly if Ridley is cut.

3yr/$45M with $32M fully guaranteed — that's an elite guarantee structure at CB. Flott started 14 games for the Giants in 2025 (38 tackles, 1 INT, 11 PBUs). This is a true cornerstone investment. Combined with the Alontae Taylor signing on the same day and Joshua Williams depth add, CB goes from the most pressing need to a position of genuine depth. CB drops from tier 1 to tier 3 or better.

3yr/$60M with $42M guaranteed — this is an elite-tier CB deal (more guaranteed than Patrick Surtain II's September 2024 extension). Taylor was a 4-year starter in New Orleans capable of playing outside and nickel. ESPN noted he is 'set to take over as the leader in the Titans' CB room.' This and the Flott signing together represent a massive CB overhaul. CB need is fully resolved.

3yr/$63M with $42M guaranteed reunites JFM with HC Robert Saleh and DL coach Aaron Whitecotton (all Jets alumni). Posted 7.5 sacks in 2025 and 7 in 2024 as a pass-rushing IDL. Fills the void left by trading Sweat and letting Joseph-Day walk. Pairs with Jeffery Simmons to form a dangerous interior tandem. IDL drops from a tier 1 need to tier 3-4. Jordan Elliott provides rotational depth alongside.

Acquired via player-for-player trade (Sweat for Johnson). Johnson was the 26th overall pick in 2022, reunites with Robert Saleh. Had 13 career sacks including 3 in 2025 after returning from a torn Achilles suffered in 2024. CRITICAL CAVEAT: This is a 1-year contract (fifth-year option, $13.41M fully guaranteed). This is a bridge, not a solution. The Titans still desperately need an EDGE they can build around long-term. Jacob Martin (2yr/$11M) adds rotational depth. EDGE remains a significant draft need.

2yr/$11M deal (guarantees TBD per reporting). Set career highs with 5.5 sacks, 18 QB hits and 14 starts for the Commanders in 2025 under Saleh's coaching tree. Speed rusher at 6-foot-2, 242 lbs. Depth/rotational contributor opposite Johnson. Does not solve the long-term EDGE starter need. EDGE remains a tier 1 draft priority.

2yr/$8M (guarantees TBD). 6-year veteran, started 16 games for the 49ers in 2025 under Saleh's defense. Fills rotational IDL depth alongside Simmons and Franklin-Myers. Does not change the need picture — IDL is already addressed by Franklin-Myers.

Acquired via trade with the Cowboys in a 7th-round pick swap (TEN gets pick #225, sends pick #218). Veteran IDL depth signing, minimal guaranteed money implied. Camp body / depth contributor. No meaningful impact on need picture.

3yr/$24M with $14.01M guaranteed — Bellinger reunites with OC Daboll who drafted him in Round 4 in 2022. He is a blocking-first TE (6'6", 255 lbs) who pairs well with starter Gunnar Helm in 12-personnel sets. However, he is not a dynamic receiving option (career-high 30 catches as a rookie, only 19 last season). Okonkwo — a far superior receiver — departed for Washington on a $30M deal. TE as a receiving weapon is only partially addressed. The in-line blocking role is filled, but a pass-catching TE2 upgrade remains a draft-viable need.

1yr deal (exact value TBD, estimated ~$3-4M based on market). Reunites with Daboll, who coached him on the Giants. Appeared in 17 games (4 starts) for NYG in 2025. Figures to compete for the starting center job vacated by Cushenberry's release. ESPN noted he could 'start at center but could get competition from second-year interior lineman Jackson Slater.' One-year prove-it deal signals this is a bridge, not a solution. IOL/center remains a draft need.

1yr/$4.215M deal per Ian Rapoport. Volson started 15 games for the Bengals in 2024 at RG but missed all of 2025 with right shoulder surgery. When healthy, is a viable RG starter. One-year deal is a low-risk, high-upside move per ESPN. IOL need reduced but not eliminated — the Titans need a long-term starting guard and center solution via the draft.

2-year deal (exact value TBD). A 4th-round pick of KC in 2022, Williams saw 15% defensive snaps in 2025 — primarily a special teams contributor. Will compete for a roster spot. With Taylor and Flott locked in as starters, Williams is CB depth. CB need is already addressed by Taylor/Flott.

2-year deal (exact terms TBD). Reunites with OC Daboll (both were together in Buffalo). Provides Cam Ward a veteran backup with scheme familiarity. Previously the #2 pick in 2017, Trubisky carries a 31-26 career record. This resolves the backup QB need cleanly. Will Levis' status in limbo now.
2yr/$6M deal per Adam Schefter. Replaces Johnny Hekker (36). Townsend spent two seasons with the Texans after four with the Chiefs. Resolves the punter need cleanly.
Re-signed on a one-year deal. Hit 80% of field goal attempts in 2025 (38 FGAs), including 9 of 14 from 50+ yards. Consistent kicker retained. K was not a need and remains addressed.
Re-signed via exclusive rights tender ($1.05M). Primarily a special teams contributor. Missed 15 games in 2025 with a knee injury. Depth/ST only — no impact on WR need picture.
One-year deal. Spent three seasons with Chiefs under GM Borgonzi's tenure there. Primarily a special teams contributor. Does not address the starter-level S vacancy left by Xavier Woods' release.

Re-signed on a one-year deal. Played 58 defensive snaps in 3 games after joining in November 2025. Will serve as the third safety behind Winston Jr. and Hooker, plus special teams. Depth only — does not resolve the S need created by Woods' departure.
One-year deal. An UDFA who started 9 games for the Jets in 2025. Has CB/S versatility. Rotational depth in a Saleh-connected signing. Does not resolve starting S vacancy.
Perennial one-year re-sign as long snapper. One of the best in the league. LS was not a need.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: The Titans executed the most aggressive CB overhaul of any team in free agency. Alontae Taylor (3yr/$60M, $42M gtd) and Cor'Dale Flott (3yr/$45M, $32M fully gtd) are both legitimate 3-year investments at starter money. Joshua Williams adds depth. CB goes from the most critical need to a position of genuine strength. No draft pick needed at CB unless it's a Day 3 value.

John Franklin-Myers (3yr/$63M, $42M gtd) fills the T'Vondre Sweat void and then some — he's a proven pass rusher (7.5 sacks in 2025) who reunites with Saleh. Elliott (2yr/$8M) and Thomas (trade) provide depth. The Simmons + Franklin-Myers tandem is elite. IDL is no longer a draft need.

Wan'Dale Robinson (4yr/$70M, $38M gtd) is a legitimate WR1 for Cam Ward in the slot — 92/1,014/4 in 2025 with Daboll. However, he is a slot specialist, and the team still lacks a true outside WR. If Calvin Ridley is released (widely expected), the WR room has Robinson, Elic Ayomanor, and Chimere Dike as the top options. The Titans should still target a complementary outside WR in rounds 2-4 of the draft.

Jermaine Johnson (1yr/$13.41M, fully gtd) is a bridge — this is his fifth-year option, the final year of his deal. He's not a franchise EDGE. Arden Key left for the Colts. Jacob Martin (2yr/$11M) is a rotational depth piece. The Titans still have no EDGE signed beyond 2026. With the #4 pick in the draft, EDGE remains a genuine tier 1 need. The Johnson acquisition helps short-term but does not change the long-term need calculus.

Center Lloyd Cushenberry was released after failing a physical. Austin Schlottmann (1yr, depth-tier deal) is a bridge starter at C with no long-term security. Cordell Volson (1yr/$4.2M) is a starting-caliber RG but coming off shoulder surgery and on a prove-it deal. Both are one-year gambits. The Titans need a long-term center and/or guard solution via the draft. IOL need unchanged at tier 2.

Chig Okonkwo departed to Washington (3yr/$30M). Daniel Bellinger (3yr/$24M, $14M gtd) replaces him as the #2 TE. Bellinger is a blocker-first, not a receiving threat. Gunnar Helm starts at TE1. The receiving component that Okonkwo provided is not adequately replaced. TE remains a moderate need — a pass-catching TE2/3 via the mid-rounds of the draft would be valuable.

Xavier Woods was released. The Titans signed three depth/ST safeties (Herring, Reed, Adams) but none is a legitimate starter. Kevin Winston Jr. and Amani Hooker are set as the top two, but Woods provided veteran starter depth that is not replaced. S need has been escalated by the depth departures. The Titans should target a starting-caliber safety in rounds 3-5 of the draft.
No significant LB moves in free agency. Cedric Gray is the incumbent starter. No notable LB departures or signings. Need unchanged.
No significant OT additions in free agency. JC Latham and Dan Moore (restructured) remain the starters. No need change.

key_additions.headline: Elite CB deal (more guaranteed than DPOY Patrick Surtain II) signals Taylor as the leader of a fully rebuilt secondary
Former Giants starter joins Taylor to give Tennessee one of the most aggressively rebuilt CB rooms of the offseason
Reunites with HC Robert Saleh; 7.5 sacks in 2025 gives Titans a true pass-rush threat alongside Jeffery Simmons
92-catch, 1,014-yard slot specialist reunites with OC Daboll and instantly becomes Cam Ward's WR1
Acquired for T'Vondre Sweat; former 1st-round pick reunites with Saleh, but it's a one-year bridge at EDGE
Blocking-first TE reunites with Daboll to pair with Gunnar Helm; replaces Okonkwo's role differently

key_losses.headline: Titans' most dynamic receiving TE (194 catches, 2,017 career yards) departs; replaced by blocking-first Bellinger
Reliable rotational edge rusher (4 sacks, 33 pressures in 2025) joins AFC South rival Colts
4yr/$76.4M bust (12 games played over 2 seasons) released; Titans pivot to Taylor and Flott at CB
Injury-plagued WR (303 yards in 7 games in 2025, broken fibula) widely expected to be released
Starting center released in February after failing a physical, opening a hole at the position

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded to Jets for Jermaine Johnson
key_losses.headline: 2024 2nd-round pick traded away in scheme-fit swap; didn't fit Saleh's defense
